2604.07802.title: Latent Anomaly Knowledge Excavation: Unveiling Sparse Sensitive Neurons in Vision-Language Models
2604.07802.paper_summary: Large-scale vision-language models (VLMs) exhibit remarkable zero-shot capabilities, yet the internal mechanisms driving their anomaly detection (AD) performance remain poorly understood. Current methods predominantly treat VLMs as black-box feature extractors, assuming that anomaly-specific knowledge must be acquired through external adapters or memory banks. In this paper, we challenge this assumption by arguing that anomaly knowledge is intrinsically embedded within pre-trained models but remains latent and under-activated. We hypothesize that this knowledge is concentrated within a sparse subset of anomaly-sensitive neurons. To validate this, we propose latent anomaly knowledge excavation (LAKE), a training-free framework that identifies and elicits these critical neuronal signals using only a minimal set of normal samples. By isolating these sensitive neurons, LAKE constructs a highly compact normality representation that integrates visual structural deviations with cross-modal semantic activations. Extensive experiments on industrial AD benchmarks demonstrate that LAKE achieves state-of-the-art performance while providing intrinsic, neuron-level interpretability. Ultimately, our work advocates for a paradigm shift: redefining anomaly detection as the targeted activation of latent pre-trained knowledge rather than the acquisition of a downstream task.
2604.07802.paper_summary_zh: 大型視覺-語言模型（VLMs）展現出卓越的零-shot 能力，但驅動其異常檢測（AD）性能的內部機制仍然不甚了解。當前的方法主要將 VLMs 視為黑箱特徵提取器，假設異常特定知識必須通過外部適配器或記憶庫來獲得。在本文中，我們挑戰這一假設，主張異常知識本質上嵌入於預訓練模型中，但仍然潛在且未被充分激活。我們假設這些知識集中在一小部分對異常敏感的神經元中。為了驗證這一點，我們提出潛在異常知識挖掘（LAKE），這是一個無需訓練的框架，僅使用一組最小的正常樣本來識別和引發這些關鍵神經信號。通過隔離這些敏感神經元，LAKE 構建了一個高度緊湊的正常性表示，將視覺結構偏差與跨模態語義激活整合在一起。在工業 AD 基準上的大量實驗表明，LAKE 實現了最先進的性能，同時提供內在的神經元級可解釋性。最終，我們的工作倡導一種範式轉變：將異常檢測重新定義為潛在預訓練知識的有針對性激活，而不是下游任務的獲取。
2604.07802.author: Shaotian Li et.al.
2604.07802.authors: Shaotian Li, Shangze Li, Chuancheng Shi, Wenhua Wu, Yanqiu Wu, Xiaohan Yu, Fei Shen, Tat-Seng Chua
